title: As I Lay Dying

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents for As I Lay Dying by William Faulkner'

memes.label: Addie Bundren
memes.description: The dying mother whose corpse the Bundren family transports to Jefferson, and whose single monologue chapter reveals the novel's deepest meditation on language, the body, and the failure of love in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: "Addie's Coffin"
memes.description: The coffin Cash Bundren builds for his dying mother — the novel's central symbolic object, simultaneously a work of craft, an act of love, and an increasingly grotesque physical presence

memes.label: "Addie Bundren's Death"
memes.description: The death of Addie Bundren — the central event that sets the entire journey in motion, witnessed differently by each family member

memes.label: Addie's Quilt
memes.description: The quilt covering Addie Bundren's dying and dead body in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, functioning as the novel's most persistent threshold between life and death, visibility and concealment.

memes.label: Anse Bundren
memes.description: The patriarch of the Bundren family — a man of stubborn laziness whose fulfillment of his promise to bury Addie in Jefferson coincides suspiciously with his acquisition of new false teeth and a new wife

memes.label: Anse's Teeth
memes.description: The set of false teeth Anse Bundren acquires in Jefferson at the end of Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, symbolizing the selfishness that has driven the journey from its beginning.

memes.label: Armstid
memes.description: A yeoman farmer who provides shelter and a team of mules to the Bundrens after the river crossing; his single narrative chapter articulates the reluctant charity and exasperated wonder that Anse Bundren inspires in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Blood as Motif
memes.description: The recurring motif of blood in *As I Lay Dying* — representing kinship, violence, mortality, and the visceral physicality of human existence.

memes.label: Body and Physicality
memes.description: Pages concerning the body, physical sensation, and embodied experience in As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Bundren Farm
memes.description: The Bundren family's hillside farm in Yoknapatawpha County, Mississippi — the novel's point of departure, where Addie dies and the journey begins

memes.label: Bundren Farmhouse
memes.description: The Bundren family's physical dwelling — distinct from bundren-farm (the land) — where Addie lies dying in her bed, Cash builds her coffin outside the window, and the family's private grief unfolds before the journey begins

memes.label: The Bundren Wagon
memes.description: The Bundren family's wagon that carries Addie's coffin from the farm to Jefferson — the central vehicle of the novel's journey narrative.

memes.label: The Buzzards
memes.description: The circling vultures that follow the Bundren wagon throughout the journey — Faulkner's recurring symbol of death's presence and the decaying body of Addie.

memes.label: Cash Bundren
memes.description: The eldest Bundren son, a carpenter whose meticulous craftsmanship and stoic endurance define his role in the family journey in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Cash's Broken Leg
memes.description: The injury sustained by Cash Bundren during the river crossing, subsequently re-broken and set in concrete by Anse — the novel's most sustained image of care gone grotesquely wrong in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: "Cash's Tools"
memes.description: Cash Bundren's carpenter's tools — the adze, saw, and plane that are extensions of his identity and the instruments through which he expresses a devotion he cannot speak

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "To **Hal Smith**"

memes.label: Class and Society
memes.description: Economic class and social hierarchy in Faulkner's Mississippi — the Bundrens as rural poor, the judgment of townspeople, and the economic realities that shape every character's choices in As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Commerce and Exchange
memes.description: The theme of commercial exchange and economic transaction as a governing logic of human relationships in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying; from Anse's relentless bargaining to Dewey Dell's failed transaction in Jefferson.

memes.label: Consciousness
memes.description: The novel's stream-of-consciousness narrative technique — interiority and perception as the defining structural principle of Faulkner's modernist method in As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Cora Tull
memes.description: A devoutly religious neighbor of the Bundren family and one of the novel's fifteen narrators, Cora Tull provides a morally judgmental perspective on the Bundrens' actions, regularly visiting Addie during her final weeks and interpreting events through the lens of Christian duty and divine will.

memes.label: Core Themes
memes.description: Thematic concepts ranked by reference frequency across As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Craft and Making
memes.description: The theme of craftsmanship embodied in Cash Bundren's coffin-building — physical labor as expression of love, grief, and moral order.

memes.label: Darl Bundren
memes.description: The most narratively dominant Bundren son, whose clairvoyant perception and eventual committal to the asylum form the novel's central arc in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Darl Burns the Barn
memes.description: Darl Bundren's attempt to destroy his mother's coffin by setting fire to the barn where it is stored — the journey's climactic act of rebellion against the grotesque funeral procession

memes.label: Darl's Committal to Jackson
memes.description: The arrest and committal of Darl Bundren to the state asylum in Jackson following his arson of Gillespie's barn — the novel's climactic act of family betrayal in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Death and Dying
memes.description: The central existential theme in As I Lay Dying — how characters confront, interpret, and are transformed by death

memes.label: Death and Mortality
memes.description: Pages concerning death, dying, and mortality across As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Death and Mourning
memes.description: Death as the novel's central event and mourning as its central action — Addie's dying, the journey to bury her, and each character's distinct mode of grief in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Dewey Dell Bundren
memes.description: The only Bundren daughter and a narrative voice in William Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, whose secret pregnancy drives her private journey parallel to the family's public funeral procession.

memes.label: Dewey Dell's Fan
memes.description: The paper fan Dewey Dell uses to cool her dying mother Addie, whose rhythmic motion becomes a hypnotic presence throughout the novel's opening chapters in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Dust
memes.description: The motif of dust in As I Lay Dying — the dry particulate matter of the Mississippi roads before the rains, carrying biblical echoes of mortality ("dust to dust") and functioning as a sensory register of the rural Southern landscape and the body's ultimate fate.

memes.label: Eula
memes.description: Minor character in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, a young woman keeping vigil at Addie Bundren's bedside who watches Darl pass through the room.

memes.label: Family and Kinship
memes.description: The Bundren family as a web of obligation, resentment, and blood — Faulkner's exploration of kinship as both bond and burden in As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Family Duty
memes.description: The moral imperative of family obligation that drives the Bundrens' journey to Jefferson

memes.label: Fire
memes.description: The motif of fire in As I Lay Dying — most dramatically realized in Darl's burning of Gillespie's barn — as an element of purification, destruction, and madness that crystallizes the novel's exploration of rationality's limits and the destructive potential of love.

memes.label: Gender and Power
memes.description: The gendered dynamics of power, voice, and agency in the rural South of As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Gender and Sexuality
memes.description: The representation of female experience, sexuality, and reproductive bodies in Faulkner's rural Mississippi — centering on Dewey Dell's pregnancy and Addie Bundren's marital discontent.

memes.label: Gillespie
memes.description: The farmer whose barn Darl burns with Addie's coffin inside; his threatened lawsuit forces the Bundrens to choose between facing legal consequences and committing Darl to the asylum in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Gillespie's Barn
memes.description: The barn where Darl sets a fire in a desperate attempt to destroy Addie's decomposing coffin, resulting in his arrest and committal to the asylum in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Identity and Selfhood
memes.description: The modernist exploration of identity as fragmented, interior, and unstable — Faulkner's rendering of consciousness as the ground of selfhood in As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Isolation
memes.description: The profound isolation of individual consciousness in As I Lay Dying — each character trapped within their own perception, unable to truly reach others

memes.label: Jefferson
memes.description: The county seat of Yoknapatawpha County and the Bundren family's destination — the town where Addie's people are buried and where the journey's multiple purposes converge

memes.label: Jewel Bundren
memes.description: The third Bundren son, Addie's illegitimate child by Whitfield — a figure of furious physical action whose wordless devotion to his mother is expressed entirely through the body

memes.label: "Jewel's Horse"
memes.description: The horse Jewel purchases with his own labor — a symbol of his fierce independence, his wordless capacity for love, and his otherness within the Bundren family

memes.label: Jody
memes.description: A drugstore clerk in Jefferson who witnesses and half-participates in Skeet Macgowan's exploitation of Dewey Dell Bundren in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Kate
memes.description: Minor character in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, a neighbor woman who appears at Addie Bundren's bedside and accompanies Cora Tull on the wagon ride home.

memes.label: Key Objects
memes.description: Significant objects ranked by reference frequency across As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Labor
memes.description: Physical labor as expression of character and identity in the Bundren family — Cash's meticulous craftsmanship, Anse's constitutional avoidance, Jewel's furious intensity, and the agrarian economy that defines their world

memes.label: Lafe
memes.description: The farmhand who impregnates Dewey Dell Bundren and then vanishes; his absence drives one of the novel's central parallel plots as Dewey Dell carries both her secret pregnancy and his ten dollars toward a drugstore abortion in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Land
memes.description: The thematic significance of land in As I Lay Dying — as property, as identity, as the ground of rural existence, and as a visual horizon that characters repeatedly gaze across in moments of contemplation and fatalism.

memes.label: Language and Meaning
memes.description: The failure of language to capture authentic experience — Addie Bundren's radical critique of words and the novel's exploration of meaning beyond speech

memes.description: Complete index of all characters in William Faulkner's As I Lay Dying

memes.description: Complete index of all locations and settings in William Faulkner's As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Littlejohn's
memes.description: The farm and store belonging to the Littlejohn family, a way-station on the Bundrens' journey to Jefferson where local farmers gather and comment on the family's grotesque funeral procession in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Lon Quick
memes.description: A legendary local figure in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, known for being the only man to successfully catch and tame one of Flem Snopes's wild Texas ponies.

memes.label: Love
memes.description: The contested nature of love in As I Lay Dying — maternal love, marital love, divine love, and the possibility that love may be indistinguishable from its absence

memes.label: Luck and Fate
memes.description: The theme of luck and cosmic misfortune in As I Lay Dying, centered on Anse Bundren's self-identification as "a luckless man" and the novel's broader exploration of whether human suffering results from personal failing, natural contingency, or a hostile cosmic order.

memes.label: Madness and Sanity
memes.description: Darl Bundren's mental breakdown, the boundary between sanity and madness, and how the community's definition of sanity functions as social control

memes.label: Memory
memes.description: Memory as a narrative and thematic force in As I Lay Dying — how characters' recollections shape their present identity and Faulkner's modernist treatment of remembered time

memes.label: Minor Characters
memes.description: Supporting and peripheral characters in As I Lay Dying, ranked by narrative presence

memes.label: Moseley
memes.description: The Mottson druggist who refuses to provide Dewey Dell Bundren with an abortion remedy on moral and legal grounds; a narrator whose professional ethics paradoxically deny aid to a desperate young woman in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Most Cited Characters
memes.description: Characters ranked by narrative reference count across As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Motherhood
memes.description: The contested meaning of motherhood in As I Lay Dying — Addie's experience as mother, her children's divergent responses to her death, and the maternal as both bond and burden

memes.label: Mottson
memes.description: A small town along the Bundrens' route to Jefferson in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, notable as the site where the family attempts to buy medicine for Cash's broken leg.

memes.label: Mud
memes.description: The recurring motif of mud in As I Lay Dying — the transformed state of the road after the rains, representing the material resistance of the earth to human purpose, the physical degradation of the journey, and the Bundren family's progressive immersion in the elements.

memes.label: Music and Song
memes.description: The motif of music and singing in As I Lay Dying — from the rhythmic quality of Cash's sawing to the hymns of Cora's religious imagination and the novel's broader engagement with sound as a register of meaning that exceeds language — representing an alternative mode of expression in a world of unreliable words.

memes.label: Narrative Places
memes.description: Locations ranked by significance across As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Narrative Technique
memes.description: Faulkner's use of stream of consciousness, multiple narrators, and interior monologue — the defining formal innovation of As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Narrative Voices
memes.description: The fifteen narrating characters of As I Lay Dying, ranked by narrative prominence

memes.label: Neighborly Duty
memes.description: The social and moral obligation of neighbors to assist one another in times of crisis in Faulkner's rural Mississippi.

memes.label: New Hope
memes.description: A rural church and community three miles from the Bundren farm, repeatedly considered as an alternative burial site for Addie Bundren in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Doctor Peabody
memes.description: The country doctor who attends Addie Bundren's death and later treats Cash's broken leg; a voice of medical and social realism amid the Bundrens' journey in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Perception and Knowledge
memes.description: How characters perceive, know, and fail to know each other — the epistemological theme of As I Lay Dying

memes.label: Poverty
memes.description: The economic conditions defining the Bundren family's existence — subsistence farming, material scarcity, and the social position of "poor folks" in Yoknapatawpha County

memes.label: Pride
memes.description: The destructive and defining force of pride in the characters of As I Lay Dying — particularly Addie Bundren's fierce self-sufficiency

memes.label: Promises and Obligations
memes.description: The theme of promises and obligations in As I Lay Dying — centered on Anse's promise to bury Addie in Jefferson — as an exploration of the binding force of words, the tension between duty and self-interest, and the way promises can become both sacred mandates and self-serving justifications.

memes.label: Rachel
memes.description: Samson's wife in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, whose furious response to the Bundrens' treatment of Addie's corpse provides one of the novel's most pointed feminist critiques of masculine funeral ritual.

memes.label: Rain
memes.description: The recurring motif of rain and impending storm in As I Lay Dying, functioning as both a literal obstacle to the Bundrens' journey and a symbolic force of nature, fate, and cosmic indifference.

memes.label: Religion and Faith
memes.description: The role of religious belief as a framework imposed on experience — and systematically undermined by the novel's embodied reality — in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Sacrifice
memes.description: What characters sacrifice in the course of the journey — and whether sacrifice is ever truly selfless

memes.label: Salvation
memes.description: Competing visions of salvation in As I Lay Dying — Cora's religious framework, Whitfield's hypocrisy, the Bundrens' earthly struggle, and the novel's ultimate refusal of transcendent meaning

memes.label: Samson
memes.description: A local farmer who hosts the Bundrens on the second night of their journey; his single narrative chapter provides one of the novel's most penetrating analyses of Anse Bundren's psychology and one of its most powerful indictments of patriarchal treatment of women in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Shame
memes.description: Shame as a gendered social force in the novel — Dewey Dell's sexual shame, Cora's moral judgment, and the Bundrens' social humiliation

memes.label: Silence and Voice
memes.description: The tension between speech and silence in *As I Lay Dying* — who speaks, who is silent, and what cannot be said aloud in Faulkner's polyphonic narrative.

memes.label: Sin and Judgment
memes.description: The tension between religious frameworks of sin and the novel's depiction of human action as driven by need rather than morality in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Skeet MacGowan
memes.description: The young soda-fountain clerk in Jefferson who deceives Dewey Dell Bundren by posing as a doctor and exploiting her desperation for an abortion remedy in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Sleep and Waking
memes.description: The motif of sleep, dreams, and the threshold between sleeping and waking in As I Lay Dying — a liminal state that parallels the novel's preoccupation with the boundary between life and death, consciousness and oblivion, the known and the unknowable.

memes.label: Smell and Odor
memes.description: The motif of smell and odor in As I Lay Dying — from the olfactory presence of Addie's decaying corpse to the sensory world of rural Mississippi — as a register of the body's materiality, mortality, and the limits of language to contain physical reality.

memes.label: Snopes
memes.description: The Snopes family and horses — Faulkner's recurring fictional family, referenced in As I Lay Dying through Anse's horse trade with Flem Snopes.

memes.label: Suffering
memes.description: The physical and emotional suffering endured by the Bundren family throughout their journey — from Cash's broken leg to Addie's prolonged illness to the psychological unraveling of Darl.

memes.label: Symbol Index
memes.description: Objects and animals functioning as symbols in As I Lay Dying, ranked by reference count

memes.label: Symbolic Animals
memes.description: Animals ranked by reference frequency across As I Lay Dying

memes.label: The Adze
memes.description: The adze used by Cash Bundren in building Addie's coffin — one of his primary carpentry tools alongside the saw and hammer, representing the specialized skill and patient craftsmanship that define Cash's mode of being and his distinctive form of mourning.

memes.label: The Bundren Barn
memes.description: The barn on the Bundren farm — a space of animal life, physical labor, and childhood refuge, where Jewel's relationship with his horse is centered and where the rhythms of farm work continue despite the unfolding crisis of Addie's death.

memes.label: The Bluff
memes.description: The bluff overlooking the path from the Bundren farm to the cotton-house, a geographic feature that frames the novel's opening scene and serves as a recurring spatial marker in Darl's narrative consciousness in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: The Body
memes.description: The novel's intense focus on physical bodies — dying, decaying, pregnant, broken — as sites where Faulkner's characters experience reality more directly than through language or thought.

memes.label: The Destroyed Bridge
memes.description: The bridge near the Bundren farm that has been washed out by flooding, forcing the family to attempt the dangerous river ford that nearly destroys them.

memes.label: The Bundren Wagon
memes.description: The Bundren family wagon that carries Addie's coffin across Yoknapatawpha County to Jefferson, becoming a rolling symbol of the journey's accumulating burden in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: The Buzzards
memes.description: The buzzards that follow the Bundrens' wagon, drawn by the scent of Addie's decomposing body — nature's commentary on the journey, at once practical scavengers and symbolic harbingers of death

memes.label: The Cake Episode
memes.description: A recurring subplot in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying spanning the novel from Cora Tull's failed cake sale at Addie's bedside to Anse Bundren's theft of the proceeds in Jefferson.

memes.label: The Cakes
memes.description: Cora Tull's cakes and Dewey Dell's failed transaction in Jefferson — a symbol of female economic agency, the failure of exchange, and the gendered economy of rural Mississippi

memes.label: The Cement
memes.description: The bag of cement purchased by Anse Bundren and applied as a crude homemade cast to Cash Bundren's broken leg, a symbol of misguided care, paternal failure, and the grotesque physical cost of the Bundrens' journey in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: The Jefferson Courthouse
memes.description: The courthouse in Jefferson, Mississippi — the seat of county law and the spatial embodiment of the institutional authority that the Bundren family encounters at the end of their journey, representing the impersonal structures of justice and social order that are alien to their rural existence.

memes.label: The Cow
memes.description: The Bundren family cow, whose distressed lowing and need for milking becomes Dewey Dell's physical and psychological double in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: The Dog
memes.description: Animal presence in As I Lay Dying — dogs appear as figures of loyalty, watchfulness, and mute endurance throughout the novel.

memes.label: The Flood
memes.description: The rising of the river that swells beyond its banks during the Bundrens' journey, transforming the river crossing from a practical obstacle into a symbolic trial of nature's resistance and divine agency in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: The Grotesque
memes.description: The aesthetic of the grotesque body in As I Lay Dying — the decaying corpse, Cash's broken leg, Vardaman's fish — and how physical deformation reveals spiritual truth

memes.label: The Hill
memes.description: The hill overlooking the Bundren farm and the surrounding Yoknapatawpha countryside, serving as both a geographic landmark and a symbolic vantage point for characters' interior reflections in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: The Journey
memes.description: The journey to Jefferson as the novel's organizing structure — simultaneously a funeral procession, a quest, an ordeal, and an absurd comedy

memes.label: The Lantern
memes.description: A recurring symbolic object in Darl's chapters — the lantern represents perception, knowledge, and the limits of human sight against darkness

memes.label: The Mule Team
memes.description: The Bundren family's mule team, drowned in the Yoknapatawpha River crossing, whose death becomes the journey's first great sacrifice in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: The Mule Team
memes.description: Anse Bundren's mule team that drowns during the river crossing — a pivotal loss that symbolizes the cost of the family's journey.

memes.label: The Porch
memes.description: The porch of the Bundren farmhouse — a liminal space between the domestic interior and the open land, serving as a site of observation, conversation, and the fatalistic contemplation that characterizes Anse Bundren's narrative presence.

memes.label: The River Crossing
memes.description: The Bundrens' disastrous attempt to ford the flooded river — the journey's physical and symbolic crisis, in which the wagon overturns, the mules drown, and Cash's leg is broken

memes.label: The River Crossing
memes.description: The flooded river ford where the Bundrens attempt to cross — the journey's most dangerous physical obstacle and its central symbolic threshold between the known world and the unknown

memes.label: The Road
memes.description: The road from the Bundren farm to Jefferson, Mississippi — the physical route of the family's funeral journey and a key setting for the novel's exploration of community, labor, and the passage through rural Yoknapatawpha County.

memes.label: The Rope
memes.description: The rope used during the catastrophic river crossing in As I Lay Dying — a tool of rescue and coordination that becomes central to one of the novel's most dramatic and symbolically freighted scenes, embodying the tension between human ingenuity and the indifferent force of nature.

memes.label: The Saw
memes.description: Cash Bundren's saw — the primary tool with which he builds Addie's coffin, and a recurring sound-image in the novel's opening chapters that establishes the acoustic texture of the Bundren household during the days of Addie's dying.

memes.label: The Spade Episode
memes.description: A darkly comic subplot in the final chapters of Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, in which Anse Bundren must borrow spades to dig his wife's grave in Jefferson.

memes.label: The Jefferson Square
memes.description: The town square of Jefferson, Mississippi — the novel's urban endpoint, where the Bundren family's rural journey terminates in a space of commerce, law, and social judgment that is fundamentally alien to their hill-country existence.

memes.label: The Stall
memes.description: The stall in the Bundren barn where Jewel keeps and secretly tends his horse — a space of hidden devotion, nocturnal labor, and the intense private bond between a young man and the animal that represents his unspoken love for his mother.

memes.label: The Washed-Out Bridge
memes.description: The collapsed bridge over the Yoknapatawpha River that forces the Bundren family to attempt a disastrous ford crossing in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Time
memes.description: Faulkner's modernist treatment of time as nonlinear, subjective, and resistant to clock measurement — a central structural principle of the novel

memes.label: Uncle Billy Varner
memes.description: The local horse doctor and veterinarian who helps set Cash Bundren's broken leg with folk medical knowledge; a voice of rural pragmatism in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Vardaman Bundren
memes.description: The youngest Bundren child, whose narrative voice refracts the trauma of his mother Addie's death through a child's associative consciousness in William Faulkner's As I Lay Dying.

memes.label: Vardaman's Bananas
memes.description: The bananas Dewey Dell promises to buy for Vardaman in Faulkner's As I Lay Dying, a recurring motif of childhood consolation that bookends the novel's journey from loss to hollow fulfillment.

memes.label: "Vardaman's Fish"
memes.description: The fish Vardaman catches and cleans on the day of his mother's death — the animal through which a child processes the incomprehensible fact of mortality and transformation

memes.label: Vernon Tull
memes.description: A neighboring farmer and one of the novel's fifteen narrators, Vernon Tull provides an outsider's perspective on the Bundren family's journey, helping them cross the flooded river while reflecting on the obligations and limits of neighborly duty.

memes.label: Water and the River
memes.description: The river as the novel's central natural symbol — simultaneously obstacle, cleansing force, agent of destruction, and border between the worlds of the living and the dead

memes.label: Whitfield
memes.description: The minister who conducted an adulterous affair with Addie Bundren and fathered Jewel; his single narrative chapter is a devastating portrait of religious self-deception, as he interprets every obstacle to his planned confession as divine providence.

memes.label: Wind
memes.description: The motif of wind in As I Lay Dying — an invisible but pervasive natural force that shapes the novel's sensory landscape, carries the smell of Addie's decaying corpse, and functions as a register of the natural world's indifference to human suffering.

memes.label: Work and Craft
memes.description: The dignity and limits of physical work in As I Lay Dying — Cash's coffin-making as the novel's central image of craft, and the relationship between work, identity, and love

memes.label: Yoknapatawpha County
memes.description: Faulkner's fictional Mississippi county — the setting of As I Lay Dying and the broader Yoknapatawpha cycle of novels and stories

memes.label: The Yoknapatawpha River
memes.description: The river the Bundrens must ford after the bridge is destroyed — site of the catastrophic wagon crossing where the mules drown and the coffin is nearly lost.
